meta.title: Wild Card vs Packers: Drive Survival -> Execution
meta.subtitle: Five decisive fourth-quarter Bears offensive plays showing how one survival rep enabled the execution cascade.
meta.game: Chicago Bears vs Green Bay Packers
meta.default_panel_text: Click a timestamp to see how the drive survived and turned into execution.

plays.detail_text: Facing fourth-and-eight down 11, Williams rolls left under pressure and finds Rome Odunze for a 27-yard conversion. The play raises Chicago's post-play win probability to 11.8% (+7.8% WPA). More importantly, it prevents the drive from ending and becomes the survival rep that makes the comeback sequence possible.
plays.significance_note: This was the survival play. Without it, the drive ends immediately.

plays.detail_text: With the drive still alive, Williams hits Olamide Zaccheaus for an 8-yard touchdown over the middle. The play pushes Chicago's post-play win probability to 20.4% (+11.2% WPA). It marks the point where the offense moves from broken-play survival back into structured execution.
plays.significance_note: The offense is now operating within structure again.

plays.detail_text: After a defensive stop and missed Packers field goal, Williams finds Colston Loveland for 12 yards. The completion lifts Chicago's post-play win probability to 43.6% (+8.2% WPA). It sustains rhythm, keeps the drive on schedule, and avoids forcing another recovery rep.
plays.significance_note: This play sustains rhythm and keeps the drive on schedule.

plays.detail_text: Now trailing by three, Williams gets the ball to D'Andre Swift for a 23-yard gain into scoring range. The play lifts Chicago's post-play win probability to 56.8% (+15.5% WPA). It flips field position and puts the defense in a far more fragile state before the decisive call arrives.
plays.significance_note: This gain flips field position and pushes Chicago into scoring range.

plays.detail_text: On the next snap, Chicago hits DJ Moore for a 25-yard touchdown on a screen-and-go concept. The play lifts Chicago's post-play win probability to 75.4% (+18.5% WPA). It is the kill shot, but it only exists because the fourth-and-eight survival play kept the drive alive.
plays.significance_note: This is the decisive execution play that completes the comeback.
